index.title: Bot Commander Help
index.metaDesc: The home of the help page of the Android based bot hoster for Discord

supportDiscord.title: Join the Support Discord
supportDiscord.heading: Get help from other users!
supportDiscord.text: Visit the official support Discord server and get help or just chat with others who use Bot Commander.

battery.title: Disabling Battery Optimizations
battery.whyText: Some different manufacturer's flavors of Android can be really strict with how they manage battery and lifetime of apps. This could cause your bot to be not responsive once you exit the application. Usually this can be solved by disabling battery optimizations, although there could be more to stop your OS from killing your apps. To find out if you need to do more it is a good idea to do research on Google searching how to stop apps from being killed on your specific device.
battery.howText: Visit this website and select your device maker. From there look at the "solution for users" section on how to stop the app from being killed.

importExport.title: Importing and Exporting
importExport.exportText: You can export a command by pressing the "Export" button when you have a command selected in the command list. This will copy a JSON string to your clipboard that contains all the information about your command.
importExport.importText: To import a command, go to the add new command area. From here, you'll select the "Import" command type and paste in your JSON string from exporting.

intents.title: Enable Gateway Intents
intents.whyText: Some newer changes to the Discord API require you to enable certain intents to recieve certain actions on your bot. Some actions like member join and member leave won't work without a certain intent enabled on the Discord developer site.
intents.whyText2: If you don't want to bother with this you can go into the settings on Bot Commander and check "Disable Server Member Intent" and you will not need to enable it for your bot. The downside of doing this will make it so Member Join, Leave, and Ban command won't function.
intents.howText: Go to your bot on the Discord developer site and scroll down to Privaleged Gateway Intents. Enable the Server Members Intent. This will allow your bot to be hosted on the app.

russian.title: Russian Note
russian.creditText: Thanks to PadowYT2 for taking the time to translate the contents of the app into Russian making Bot Commander more accessible.
russian.noteText: Если нужна помощь по приложение, пожалуйста зайдите на сервер и спросите в канале #non-english-support . Буду рад помочь. -S | PadowYT2 (Server Supporter, App Supporter)

items: Add persistent state to allow bots to remember information between sessions.
Fixed various bugs.
Fixed various bugs.
Help site theming changed to be neutral colored.
Fixed various bugs.
Commands are now also updated while hosting, this means you will not have to restart the bot in order to get and test your latest changes in your bot.
Status changes are now also updated while hosting.
Furthermore, new status options have been added. You can now set the online status (Online, Idle, Do Not Disturb, Invisible) as well as some other activity statuses such as "Competing"

The import / export system has been overhauled. The old one was quite obtuse and hard to use, so it has been replaced with a simpler and easier to use system. When in the command list, you can click the export button on a command and it will copy to your clipboard. To import, you go to the add command menu and set the type to import, and paste in the command you wish to import.
Various new keywords have been added related to time and members. These include some keywords such as $hour, $memberTimeJoined, $memberHasBoosted, and more.

The UI has been updated to Material 3. In general this gives the app a new and more modern look. A new addition included with this for users with devices which support it is Dynamic Color. With Dynamic Color, the app's UI will match that of the pallete of your device.
Ads have been completely removed from the app. You will no longer see ads in the help section.
Fixed a bug where if you entered and tried to sign in with an incorrect token, the app would crash.
Fixed a bug where some keywords were not showing up in the keyword searcher.
Enabled the MESSAGE_CONTENT gateway intent.
Updated Important Libraries.
Added $setStatus keyword.
Fixed some crashes.
Added a keyword searcher in the create command menu.
Improved the command search bar.
Various fixes and small changes.
Removed language selection, instead left up to system.
Various fixes.
Russian Language, thanks to PadowYT2.
Japanese Language
Added language selection in settings.
Added the ability to use webhooks.
$sum keyword.
Various fixes.
Color for embeds.
Bug fixes.
Added "require administrator privilege" for commands.
Added option to hide output in command list.
Added option to remove the need to enable server member intents at the cost of not being able to use some commands.
Performance and bug fixes
Added search bar to command list.
Added ability to swipe either direction to delete a command.
Added option to hide token text.
Added Export button to export your commands in a file.
Added Import button to load commands from a file.
Fixed many bugs such as bottom navigation moving up with keyboard, some command list bugs, as well as some bot service bugs.
Revamped how the help section and the help website look.
Added and adjusted tabs for the help section.
Removed help and tutorial button options from the other menu.
Added $eval and $halt keywords to allow for the use of JavaScript.
Added $get() and $set() keywords to set and retrieve values.
Changed token field text type.
New delete actions including delete command after, delete x messages, and delete if contains.
24 new keywords have been added including $ping and $inviteURL.
Action to filter not safe for work commands.
Multi-line support for some fields.
Added require specific message ID for react add events.
Added Ignore error messages for commands.
Added $random{} keyword to select random string.
Added icon for PM receive type.
Added Require Role for commands of certain types.
Added Send in Specific Channel for commands of certain types.
Added keywords related to mentioned user info, bot info, server info, and a few more.
Added support for "Streaming..." and "Watching..." status types.
ability to send embed messages.
Added ability to react to messages.
Added help button in command creation screen.
Revamped the command creation screen for ease of use.
Added role assignment function.
Added links to tutorial and a discord in other menu.
Moderation kick, ban, voice mute. These work in a special way see here.
Wildcard keyword. * in the command field will mean anything will trigger the command.

Reaction add command type. This type is triggered by someone adding a reaction to a message. For custom emojis in the reaction add type you will use the name of the emoji (eg. your emote is named WeirdChamp, you would then put WeirdChamp), and regular emojis will just use the emoji in the command field.
Tutorial dialogs to mention some important details.
New keyword $avatar which adds the user avatar to the message.

commands.title: Commands
commands.metaDesc: All the basics of commands in Bot Commander for Discord

messageReceive.name: Message Receive
messageReceive.desc: Indicates that the command will trigger when a message is recieved in a server the bot is in.

privateMessage.name: Private Message
privateMessage.desc: Indicates that the command will trigger when a message is recieved through direct message with the bot.

memberJoin.name: Member Join
memberJoin.desc: Indicates the command will trigger when a member joins a server the bot is in.

memberLeave.name: Member Leave
memberLeave.desc: Indicates the command will trigger when a member leaves a server the bot is in.

memberBan.name: Member Ban
memberBan.desc: Indicates the command will trigger when a member is banned from a server the bot is in.

reactionAdd.name: Reaction Add
reactionAdd.desc: Indicates the command will trigger when a reaction is added to a message in a server the bot is in. Command field for this type must be filled with an emoji or name of a custom emoji to trigger, or * can be used so it always triggers.

command.name: Command
command.desc: The string (or emoji for Reaction Add) that will cause the actions of the command to happen. If * is placed in this field the command's actions will always happen.

description.name: Command Description
description.desc: An optional description you can write to remember what your command does.

phrase.name: Phrase
phrase.desc: The command string can be anywhere in the message to activate the command.

requiresRole.name: Requires Role
requiresRole.desc: The command will only activate with the given role.

requiresAdmin.name: Requires Administrator Privilege
requiresAdmin.desc: The command will only activate if the user who calls it has admin privileges.

sendChannel.name: Send Channel Message
sendChannel.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sendChannel.desc: Sends a messsage in the channel where the command is called.

sendChannelEmbed.name: Send Channel Message Embed
sendChannelEmbed.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sendChannelEmbed.desc: Creates an embed. Image URL and Thumbnail URL must be valid if used.

sendSpecificChannel.name: Send in specific channel
sendSpecificChannel.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sendSpecificChannel.desc: Overrides what channel the channel message and embed actions get sent to.

sendPrivate.name: Send Private Message
sendPrivate.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sendPrivate.desc: Sends a private message to the user who called the command.

sendPrivateEmbed.name: Send Private Message Embed
sendPrivateEmbed.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sendPrivateEmbed.desc: Creates an embed. Image URL and Thumbnail URL must be valid if used.

react.name: React to received message
react.desc: Reacts to the message that triggered the command. If you're using a custom emoji, then just use the name of it.

ignoreError.name: Ignore error messages
ignoreError.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
ignoreError.desc: Ignores most error messages that are not fatal to the functions of the command.

requireMessageId.name: Require specific message ID
requireMessageId.desc: Requires a specific message ID for the React Add type command to function.

deleteIfContains.name: Delete if contains
deleteIfContains.desc: Removes the message if it contains a certain string. Different strings must be separated with |.

deleteAfter.name: Delete command message after
deleteAfter.desc: Deletes the message that called the command.

deleteX.name: Delete X messages
deleteX.desc: Deletes the number indicated in the field amount of messsages.

nsfw.name: NSFW only
nsfw.desc: The command can only be called in NSFW channels.

kick.name: Kick
kick.desc: Kicks a user. Requires kicking permissions. Usage: if command is "kick" usage is "kick @user"

ban.name: Ban
ban.desc: Bans a user. Requires banning permissions. Usage: if command is "ban" usage is "ban @user"

voiceMute.name: Voice Mute
voiceMute.desc: Voice mutes a user if called first time, else unmutes them. Requires muting permissions. Usage: if command is "mute" usage is "mute @user"

assignRole.name: Assign/Remove Role
assignRole.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Reaction Add
assignRole.desc: Assigns a role when called the if user has the role, else it removes the role.

whatIs.name: What is it?
whatIs.desc: This is the file that is outputted when you export your commands. You could upload and send it to other people to use with the app, but you could also use these commands as the basis for a bot you decide to write yourself. I've tried to describe what each value is and what it is used for here.

actionArr.name: actionArr[bool, bool]
actionArr.desc: This is an array. The first index represents if the command should send a channel message. The second represents if a private message should be sent.

channelMessage.name: channelMessage
channelMessage.desc: The channel message that will be sent.

command.name: command
command.desc: The command that the message must equal inorder for the command to trigger. This will be an emoji or name of custom emoji for the react add type.

deleteAfter.name: deleteAfter
deleteAfter.desc: If the message that triggered the command should be deleted afterwards.

deleteIf.name: deleteIf
deleteIf.desc: A boolean that is used to determine if the command should be deleted if it contains certain strings.

commandIfStrings.name: commandIfStrings
commandIfStrings.desc: The strings that will be used if deleteIf is true to delete a message.

commandNum.name: commandNum
commandNum.desc: The number of messages to delete if deleteX is true.

deleteX.name: deleteX
deleteX.desc: If this is a mass delete command.

ignoreErrorMessage.name: ignoreErrorMessage
ignoreErrorMessage.desc: If the command should ignore error messages.

isBan.name: isBan
isBan.desc: If the command should ban someone.

isKick.name: isKick
isKick.desc: If the command should kick someone.

isNSFW.name: isNSFW
isNSFW.desc: If the command is NSFW and should only be sent in NSFW channels.

isReact.name: isReact
isReact.desc: If the command should react to the message received.

isRequiredRole.name: isRequiredRole
isRequiredRole.desc: If the command should require a role to be used.

isRoleAssigner.name: isRoleAssigner
isRoleAssigner.desc: If the command should assign a role.

isSpecificChannel.name: isSpecificChannel
isSpecificChannel.desc: If the command should send channel or embed messages into a specific channel.

isSpecificMessage.name: isSpecificMessage
isSpecificMessage.desc: If the command should, if react add type, only work on a specific message ID.

isVoiceMute.name: isVoiceMute
isVoiceMute.desc: If the command should voice mute a user.

phrase.name: phrase
phrase.desc: If the command should activate if there is more than just the command in the recieved message.

privateMessage.name: privateMessage
privateMessage.desc: The content of the private message that will send.

reaction.name: reaction
reaction.desc: The emote that will be used to react if isReact is true.

requiredRole.name: requiredRole
requiredRole.desc: The role that is required to send certain messages if isRoleRequired is true.

roleToAssign.name: roleToAssign
roleToAssign.desc: The role that will be assigned if isRoleAssigner is true.

sendChannelEmbed.name: sendChannelEmbed
sendChannelEmbed.desc: If a channel embed should be sent.

sendPrivateEmbed.name: sendPrivateEmbed
sendPrivateEmbed.desc: If a private embed should be sent.

specificChannel.name: specificChannel
specificChannel.desc: The name of the specific channel to send a message to.

specificMessage.name: specificMessage
specificMessage.desc: The ID of a certain message that must be reacted to.

channelEmbed.name: channelEmbed
channelEmbed.desc: The channel embed to be sent, includes a title, description, footer, image, and thumbnail.

privateEmbed.name: privateEmbed
privateEmbed.desc: The private embed to be sent, includes a title, description, footer, image, and thumbnail.

keywords.title: Keywords
keywords.metaDesc: Documentation of all the keywords in Bot Commander for Discord and how to use them

name.name: $name
name.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Reaction Add
name.desc: Inserts a mention of the user who called the command.

avatar.name: $avatar
avatar.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
avatar.desc: Inserts the avatar URL of the user who called the command.

defaultAvatar.name: $defaultAvatar
defaultAvatar.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
defaultAvatar.desc: Inserts the default avatar URL of the user who called the command.

namePlain.name: $namePlain
namePlain.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
namePlain.desc: Inserts the plain text username of the user who called the command.

discriminator.name: $discriminator
discriminator.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
discriminator.desc: Inserts the discriminator (the numbers after the #) of the user who called the command.

tag.name: $tag
tag.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
tag.desc: Inserts the tag (in the form Username#1234) of the user who called the command.

id.name: $ID
id.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
id.desc: Inserts the unique ID number of the user who called the command.

serversShared.name: $serversSharedWithBot
serversShared.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
serversShared.desc: Inserts the number of servers shared with the bot the user who called the command has.

timeCreated.name: $timeCreated
timeCreated.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
timeCreated.desc: Inserts the account creation time of the user who called the command.

isBot.name: $isBot
isBot.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
isBot.desc: Returns "true" or "false" indicating whether the user who called the command is a bot.

globalName.name: $globalName
globalName.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
globalName.desc: Inserts the global display name of the user who called the command.

isOwner.name: $memberIsOwner
isOwner.desc: Is the member of the event the owner of the server.

effectiveName.name: $memberEffectiveName
effectiveName.desc: This is the name the member goes by within the server.

effectiveAvatar.name: $memberEffectiveAvatar
effectiveAvatar.desc: This is the avatar that the user has displayed within this specific server.

memberId.name: $memberID
memberId.desc: The unique ID of the member.

hasTimeJoined.name: $memberHasTimeJoined
hasTimeJoined.desc: Gives "true" or "false" denoting whether the user has a time they joined the server.

timeJoined.name: $memberTimeJoined
timeJoined.desc: The time the member joined the server.

hasBoosted.name: $memberHasBoosted
hasBoosted.desc: Has this member boosted the server?. Returns "true" or "false".

timeBoosted.name: $memberTimeBoosted
timeBoosted.desc: What is the time the user boosted the server? If there is none we will get "null".

nickname.name: $memberNickname
nickname.desc: Inserts the member's server nickname, if one is set.

effectiveTag.name: $memberEffectiveTag
effectiveTag.desc: Inserts the tag (in the form Username#1234) of the member.

effectiveId.name: $memberEffectiveID
effectiveId.desc: Inserts the unique ID of the member's underlying user account.

effectiveTimeCreated.name: $memberEffectiveTimeCreated
effectiveTimeCreated.desc: Inserts the account creation time of the member's underlying user account.

color.name: $memberColor
color.desc: Inserts the hex color code of the member's display color in the server.

roles.name: $memberRoles
roles.desc: Inserts a comma-separated list of the member's role names in the server.

roleCount.name: $memberRoleCount
roleCount.desc: Inserts the number of roles the member has in the server.

name.name: $mentionedName
name.desc: Inserts a mention of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

avatar.name: $mentionedAvatar
avatar.desc: Inserts the avatar URL of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

defaultAvatar.name: $mentionedDefaultAvatar
defaultAvatar.desc: Inserts the default avatar URL of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

namePlain.name: $mentionedNamePlain
namePlain.desc: Inserts the plain text username of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

discriminator.name: $mentionedDiscriminator
discriminator.desc: Inserts the discriminator (the numbers after the #) of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

tag.name: $mentionedTag
tag.desc: Inserts the tag (in the form Username#1234) of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

id.name: $mentionedID
id.desc: Inserts the unique ID number of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

serversShared.name: $mentionedServersSharedWithBot
serversShared.desc: Inserts the number of servers shared with the bot the user mentioned in the call of the command.

timeCreated.name: $mentionedTimeCreated
timeCreated.desc: Inserts the account creation time of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

isBot.name: $mentionedIsBot
isBot.desc: Returns "true" or "false" indicating whether the mentioned user is a bot.

globalName.name: $mentionedGlobalName
globalName.desc: Inserts the global display name of the user mentioned in the call of the command.

name.name: $botName
name.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
name.desc: Inserts a mention of your bot.

avatar.name: $botAvatar
avatar.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
avatar.desc: Inserts the avatar URL of your bot.

defaultAvatar.name: $botDefaultAvatar
defaultAvatar.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
defaultAvatar.desc: Inserts the default avatar URL of your bot.

namePlain.name: $botNamePlain
namePlain.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
namePlain.desc: Inserts the plain text username of your bot.

discriminator.name: $botDiscriminator
discriminator.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
discriminator.desc: Inserts the discriminator (the numbers after the #) of your bot.

tag.name: $botTag
tag.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
tag.desc: Inserts the tag (in the form Username#1234) of your bot.

id.name: $botID
id.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
id.desc: Inserts the unique ID number of your bot.

timeCreated.name: $botTimeCreated
timeCreated.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
timeCreated.desc: Inserts the account creation time of your bot.

server.name: $server
server.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
server.desc: Inserts the name of the server where the command was called.

serverCount.name: $serverCount
serverCount.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
serverCount.desc: Inserts the number of the servers the bot is in.

memberCount.name: $memberCount
memberCount.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
memberCount.desc: Inserts the number of users in the server where the command was called.

allMemberCount.name: $allMemberCount
allMemberCount.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
allMemberCount.desc: Inserts the number of users in all server where the bot is.

icon.name: $serverIcon
icon.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
icon.desc: Inserts the URL of the icon of the server where the command was called.

banner.name: $serverBanner
banner.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
banner.desc: Inserts the URL of the server banner of the server where the command was called.

description.name: $serverDescription
description.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
description.desc: Inserts the description of the server where the command was called.

splash.name: $serverSplash
splash.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
splash.desc: Inserts the URL of the splash of the server where the command was called.

createTime.name: $serverCreateTime
createTime.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
createTime.desc: Inserts the creation time of the server where the command was called.

serverId.name: $serverID
serverId.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
serverId.desc: Inserts the unique ID of the server where the command was called.

owner.name: $serverOwner
owner.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
owner.desc: Inserts a mention of the server owner.

ownerPlain.name: $serverOwnerPlain
ownerPlain.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
ownerPlain.desc: Inserts the plain text display name of the server owner.

boostCount.name: $serverBoostCount
boostCount.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
boostCount.desc: Inserts the number of boosts the server has.

boostTier.name: $serverBoostTier
boostTier.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
boostTier.desc: Inserts the boost tier level of the server (0, 1, 2, or 3).

vanityCode.name: $serverVanityCode
vanityCode.compat: Message Receive, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
vanityCode.desc: Inserts the vanity invite code of the server, if one exists.

channel.name: $channel
channel.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Reaction Add
channel.desc: Inserts the name of the channel where the message was sent.

channelId.name: $channelID
channelId.desc: Inserts the ID of the channel where the message was sent.

createDate.name: $channelCreateDate
createDate.desc: Inserts the time of creation of the channel where the message was sent.

asMention.name: $channelAsMention
asMention.desc: Inserts the mention of the channel where the message was sent.

topic.name: $channelTopic
topic.desc: Inserts the topic of the channel where the message was sent.

isNSFW.name: $channelIsNSFW
isNSFW.desc: Returns "true" or "false" indicating whether the channel is marked as NSFW.

createChannel.name: $createChannel(name, type)
createChannel.desc: Creates a new channel with the given name and type (text, voice, category, announcement, stage, forum). Returns the new channel's ID. Defaults to text if type is omitted.

createChannelIn.name: $createChannelIn(name, type, categoryID)
createChannelIn.desc: Creates a new channel with the given name and type inside the specified category. Returns the new channel's ID.

cloneChannel.name: $cloneChannel(channelID)
cloneChannel.desc: Clones an existing channel, copying its settings and permissions. Returns the new channel's ID.

deleteChannel.name: $deleteChannel(channelID, reason)
deleteChannel.desc: Deletes the specified channel. An optional reason can be provided for the audit log. Returns "true" on success.

setChannelName.name: $setChannelName(channelID, name)
setChannelName.desc: Changes the name of the specified channel. Returns "true" on success.

setChannelTopic.name: $setChannelTopic(channelID, topic)
setChannelTopic.desc: Sets the topic of the specified channel. Returns "true" on success.

setChannelNSFW.name: $setChannelNSFW(channelID, enabled)
setChannelNSFW.desc: Sets whether the specified channel is marked as NSFW. Use "true" or "false". Returns "true" on success.

setChannelSlowmode.name: $setChannelSlowmode(channelID, seconds)
setChannelSlowmode.desc: Sets the slowmode delay for the specified channel in seconds (0–21600). Use 0 to disable. Returns "true" on success.

setChannelPosition.name: $setChannelPosition(channelID, position)
setChannelPosition.desc: Sets the position of the specified channel in the channel list. Returns "true" on success.

setChannelParent.name: $setChannelParent(channelID, categoryID)
setChannelParent.desc: Moves the specified channel into a category. Pass an empty categoryID to remove from any category. Returns "true" on success.

findChannel.name: $findChannel(name)
findChannel.desc: Finds a channel by name (case-insensitive) and returns its ID. Returns empty string if not found.

getChannelName.name: $getChannelName(channelID)
getChannelName.desc: Returns the name of the specified channel, or empty string if not found.

getChannelType.name: $getChannelType(channelID)
getChannelType.desc: Returns the type of the specified channel (text, voice, category, announcement, stage, or forum).

getChannelParent.name: $getChannelParent(channelID)
getChannelParent.desc: Returns the ID of the parent category of the specified channel, or empty string if none.

channelCount.name: $channelCount
channelCount.desc: Returns the total number of channels in the server.

listChannels.name: $listChannels(type)
listChannels.desc: Returns a comma-separated list of channel names. Optionally filter by type (text, voice, category, etc.).

listChannelIDs.name: $listChannelIDs(type)
listChannelIDs.desc: Returns a comma-separated list of channel IDs. Optionally filter by type (text, voice, category, etc.).

lockChannel.name: $lockChannel(channelID, roleID)
lockChannel.desc: Locks a channel by denying Send Messages for a role. Defaults to @everyone if roleID is omitted. Returns "true" on success.

unlockChannel.name: $unlockChannel(channelID, roleID)
unlockChannel.desc: Unlocks a channel by resetting Send Messages permission for a role. Defaults to @everyone if roleID is omitted. Returns "true" on success.

channelMention.name: $channelMention(channelID)
channelMention.desc: Returns a clickable channel mention for the specified channel ID (e.g. <#123456>).

message.name: $message
message.desc: Inserts the whole message of the called command.

afterCommand.name: $messageAfterCommand
afterCommand.desc: Inserts the message after the command part of the called command.

args.name: $args(index)
args.desc: Inserts a specific argument from the message by its index (0-based).

argsCount.name: $argsCount
argsCount.desc: Inserts the number of arguments in the message.

option.name: $option(name)
option.desc: Inserts the value of a slash command option by its name.

eval.name: $eval
eval.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
eval.desc: Used to mark the start of a js code block. Must be followed by a $halt statement after the code is finished

halt.name: $halt
halt.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
halt.desc: Used to mark the end of a js code block.

get.name: $get(name)
get.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
get.desc: Inserts the value of the js variable with that name.

set.name: $set(name,value)
set.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
set.desc: Used to set a js variable. Supports boolean "true" and "false" as well as numbers and strings.

date.name: $date
date.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

hour.name: $hour
hour.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

minute.name: $minute
minute.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

second.name: $second
second.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

random.name: $random{a|b|c}
random.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
random.desc: Inserts a randomly selected string from the arguments located in the brackets {}. Strings must be separated using |.

sum.name: $sum{1|2|3}
sum.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sum.desc: Inserts the sum of its arguments located in the brackets {}. Numbers must be separated using |.

commandCount.name: $commandCount
commandCount.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
commandCount.desc: Inserts the amount of commands the bot has.

rollnum.name: $rollnum(min,max)
rollnum.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
rollnum.desc: Inserts a random number between min and max inclusive.

ping.name: $ping
ping.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

inviteUrl.name: $inviteURL
inviteUrl.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
inviteUrl.desc: Inserts URL to invite the bot to a server.

setStatus.name: $setStatus{online|playing|games}
setStatus.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
setStatus.desc: Use to set status in commands. Values for first parameter include online, idle, dnd. Second parameter values are watching, playing, listening. The third parameter is the string of whatever. Important note is this is evaluated before $eval so you can not use $eval type keywords this.

randomInt.name: $randomInt
randomInt.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
randomInt.desc: Inserts a random integer between 0 and 99.

randomFloat.name: $randomFloat
randomFloat.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
randomFloat.desc: Inserts a random decimal number between 0 and 1.

randomBoolean.name: $randomBoolean
randomBoolean.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

chat.name: $chat(prompt)
chat.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
chat.desc: Sends the given prompt to an AI and inserts the response.

upper.name: $upper(text)
upper.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
upper.desc: Converts the given text to uppercase.

lower.name: $lower(text)
lower.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
lower.desc: Converts the given text to lowercase.

length.name: $length(text)
length.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
length.desc: Returns the number of characters in the given text.

replace.name: $replace(text,find,replacement)
replace.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
replace.desc: Replaces all occurrences of "find" with "replacement" in the given text.

substring.name: $substring(text,start,end)
substring.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
substring.desc: Extracts a portion of the text from the start index to the end index (0-based).

trim.name: $trim(text)
trim.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
trim.desc: Removes whitespace from the beginning and end of the given text.

repeat.name: $repeat(text,count)
repeat.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
repeat.desc: Repeats the given text a specified number of times (max 100).

contains.name: $contains(text,search)
contains.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
contains.desc: Returns "true" if the text contains the search string, "false" otherwise. Case-sensitive.

startsWith.name: $startsWith(text,prefix)
startsWith.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
startsWith.desc: Returns "true" if the text starts with the given prefix, "false" otherwise. Case-sensitive.

endsWith.name: $endsWith(text,suffix)
endsWith.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
endsWith.desc: Returns "true" if the text ends with the given suffix, "false" otherwise. Case-sensitive.

sub.name: $sub(a,b)
sub.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sub.desc: Subtracts b from a and returns the result.

mul.name: $mul(n1,n2,...)
mul.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
mul.desc: Multiplies all given numbers together and returns the result.

div.name: $div(a,b)
div.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
div.desc: Divides a by b and returns the result. Returns an error if b is zero.

mod.name: $mod(a,b)
mod.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
mod.desc: Returns the remainder of dividing a by b. Returns an error if b is zero.

round.name: $round(n,decimals)
round.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
round.desc: Rounds n to the nearest integer, or to the specified number of decimal places if provided.

floor.name: $floor(n)
floor.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
floor.desc: Rounds n down to the nearest integer.

ceil.name: $ceil(n)
ceil.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
ceil.desc: Rounds n up to the nearest integer.

abs.name: $abs(n)
abs.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
abs.desc: Returns the absolute value of n.

toFixed.name: $toFixed(n,decimals)
toFixed.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
toFixed.desc: Formats n to a fixed number of decimal places (default 2, max 20).

min.name: $min(n1,n2,...)
min.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
min.desc: Returns the smallest value from the given numbers.

max.name: $max(n1,n2,...)
max.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
max.desc: Returns the largest value from the given numbers.

clamp.name: $clamp(n,min,max)
clamp.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
clamp.desc: Clamps n so that it is no less than min and no greater than max.

pow.name: $pow(base,exp)
pow.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
pow.desc: Returns base raised to the power of exp.

sqrt.name: $sqrt(n)
sqrt.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
sqrt.desc: Returns the square root of n. Returns an error if n is negative.

log.name: $log(n)
log.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
log.desc: Returns the natural logarithm of n. Returns an error if n is zero or negative.

pi.name: $pi
pi.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add

isNumber.name: $isNumber(text)
isNumber.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
isNumber.desc: Returns "true" if the text is a valid number, "false" otherwise.

if.name: $if(condition)
if.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
if.desc: Starts a conditional block. Content between $if and $endif (or $else/$elseif) is only included if the condition is true. Supports operators: == (equals), != (not equals), > < >= <= (numeric comparison), & (and), | (or), ! (not), and () for grouping. A value is true if it is not empty, not "false", and not "0".

elseif.name: $elseif(condition)
elseif.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
elseif.desc: Adds an alternative branch to an $if block. If the previous condition was false, this condition is checked. You can chain multiple $elseif blocks.

else.name: $else
else.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
else.desc: Adds a fallback branch to an $if block. The content after $else is used when all previous conditions were false.

endif.name: $endif
endif.compat: Message Receive, Private Message, Member Join, Member Leave, Member Ban, Reaction Add
endif.desc: Ends an $if block. Every $if must have a matching $endif.

creating.title: Starting out with the bot
creating.metaDesc: How to create and set up a bot for the Bot Commander for Discord app

creatingBot.title: Creating the bot
creatingBot.step2: 2. Once on the dashboard click the "New Application" button.
creatingBot.step3: 3. Name the application whatever you want.
creatingBot.step4: 4. In the app dashboard press the menu button and select "Bot"
creatingBot.step6: 6. Now you can copy your token and change other aspects of the bot account.
creatingBot.step7: 7. IMPORTANT scroll and enable Server Members Intent on your bot. If you don't your bot may not function properly or host at all in the app.

invitingBot.title: Inviting the bot
invitingBot.step1: Inviting the bot is also done from here!
invitingBot.step2: Click on the OAuth2 button from the menu
invitingBot.step4: Scroll down and select your bot permissions, "Administrator" counts for all permissions if you need them all."
invitingBot.step5: Scroll back up to the scopes and copy then open the link. Note: You must have the "Manage Server" permission in the server you wish to add the bot to.

firstCommand.title: Your First Command
firstCommand.heading: Getting Started
firstCommand.intro: Let's create your first bot command. We'll make a simple greeting that responds when someone types !hello in your server.
firstCommand.step1: Open Bot Commander and go to the Commands tab.
firstCommand.step2: Tap the + button to create a new command.
firstCommand.step5: In the channel message field, enter the response template shown below.
firstCommand.step6: Save the command, host your bot, and type !hello in a Discord channel to test it.

usingKeywords.title: Using Keywords
usingKeywords.heading: Variables & Functions
usingKeywords.intro: Keywords let you insert dynamic information into your bot's responses. Variables like $name and $server are replaced with real values, and functions like $rollnum() and $random{} generate content on the fly.
usingKeywords.step2: Enter the response template below in the channel message field.
usingKeywords.step3: Save, host your bot, and type !info to see the keywords in action.

slashCommands.title: Slash Commands
slashCommands.heading: Interaction Commands
slashCommands.intro: Slash commands are Discord's modern way of interacting with bots. Users type / followed by the command name, and Discord shows autocomplete options. You can define typed parameters that Discord validates for you.
slashCommands.step1: Go to the Interactions tab and tap + to create a new interaction command.
slashCommands.step2: Set the command name to greet (no prefix needed for slash commands).
slashCommands.step4: Add an option: type String, name person, description Who to greet.
slashCommands.step5: Set the response template shown below.
slashCommands.step6: Save, host your bot, and type /greet in Discord. Fill in the person option and send it.

buttons.title: Interactive Buttons
buttons.heading: Buttons on Messages
buttons.intro: You can add clickable buttons to your slash command responses. Each button can trigger its own response, assign roles, or show ephemeral messages that only the clicker can see.
buttons.step1: Create a new interaction command named menu with the description Show the menu.
buttons.step2: Set the response message to the template shown below.
buttons.step3: Add a button with label Info, style Primary, and ID info_btn. Set its response to Here is some info about our server!
buttons.step4: Add another button with label Help, style Secondary, and ID help_btn. Set its response to Need help? Ask in #support!
buttons.step5: Optionally, check Ephemeral on the button responses so only the person who clicks sees the reply.
buttons.step6: Save, host, and test with /menu in Discord.

conditionals.title: Conditional Responses
conditionals.heading: If / Else Logic
conditionals.intro: Use $if, $elseif, $else, and $endif to make your bot respond differently based on conditions. You can check arguments, user properties, server info, and more.
conditionals.step2: Enter the conditional template below in the channel message field.
conditionals.step3: Save and test. Try !role admin, !role mod, and !role anything to see the different responses.

embeds.title: Embed Messages
embeds.heading: Rich Embeds
embeds.intro: Embeds are rich, formatted messages with a colored side bar, title, description, footer, images, and thumbnails. They make your bot's responses look polished and professional.
embeds.step3: Fill in the embed fields as shown below. All fields are optional as long as you fill in at least one.
embeds.step4: Save, host, and type !profile to see your embed.

welcome.title: Welcome Messages
welcome.heading: Member Join Events
welcome.intro: You can make your bot automatically greet new members when they join your server. Combine this with role assignment to give new members a default role.
welcome.step1: Create a new command and set the type to Member Join.
welcome.step2: Enter the response template below in the channel message field.
welcome.step3: Enable Assign/Remove Role and enter the role name (e.g. Member). This is case-sensitive.
welcome.step4: Save and host. When someone joins your server, the bot will greet them and assign the role automatically.

persistState.title: Persisting State
persistState.heading: Saving Data Between Sessions
persistState.intro: The botState object lets you store data that persists even when you stop and restart the bot. You can modify it inside $eval...$halt blocks and read its properties with $get(). You can also use $set() and $get() outside of eval blocks to store and retrieve values on the VM context directly.
persistState.step2: Enter the template below. The $eval block runs JavaScript and its return value replaces the block in the message. The botState object persists across bot restarts.
persistState.step3: Save and test. The counter increments each time and survives bot restarts.

channels.title: Channel Management
channels.heading: Managing Channels
channels.intro: Bot Commander includes functions for creating, deleting, and managing Discord channels directly from your command responses. Your bot needs the Manage Channels permission for these to work.
channels.step2: Enter the template below. It creates a new text channel and lists all channels in the server.
channels.step3: Save and test. Make sure your bot has the Manage Channels permission in your server.

aiChat.title: AI-Powered Responses
aiChat.heading: Using AI in Commands
aiChat.intro: The $chat() function sends a prompt to AI and returns the response. Combine it with $messageAfterCommand to let users ask your bot anything. You'll need an API key configured in your bot settings.
aiChat.step2: Enter one of the response templates below. The simple version passes the user's question directly to AI. The advanced version adds formatting.
aiChat.step3: Save and test. Type !ask followed by any question.

triggerModes.title: Trigger Modes
triggerModes.heading: Command Only, Starts With & Phrase
triggerModes.intro: When you create a "Message Received" command, you can choose how the bot matches incoming messages. By default the message must exactly match your command name. The Starts With and Phrase options change this behavior.
triggerModes.commandOnlyDesc: The message must be exactly the command name, nothing more. For example, a command named !hello will only trigger when someone types exactly !hello. Typing !hello world or say !hello will not trigger it.
triggerModes.startsWithDesc: Enable Starts With to trigger the command when a message begins with the command name. This is useful for commands that accept arguments. Use $args(0), $args(1), etc. to access the words after the command, or $messageAfterCommand to get everything after the command name.
triggerModes.phraseDesc: Enable Phrase to trigger the command whenever the command name appears anywhere in a message. The match is case-insensitive. This is great for auto-responses to certain words or topics.

moderation.title: Moderation Commands
moderation.heading: Kick, Ban & Voice Mute
moderation.intro: Bot Commander has built-in moderation actions. When you enable kick, ban, or voice mute on a command, the bot performs that action on the mentioned user. The user running the command must have the matching Discord permission (Kick Members, Ban Members, or Mute Members). Usage is always: command @user.
moderation.kickDesc: Creates a command that kicks the mentioned user from the server.
moderation.kickStep2: Enable the Kick option in the moderation section.
moderation.kickStep3: Optionally add a channel message like the template below to confirm the action.
moderation.kickStep4: Save and test with !kick @user. Both you and the bot need the Kick Members permission.
moderation.banDesc: Works the same way as kick, but permanently bans the user from the server.
moderation.banStep2: Enable the Ban option in the moderation section.
moderation.banStep3: Save and test with !ban @user. Both you and the bot need the Ban Members permission.
moderation.muteDesc: Toggles server mute on a user in a voice channel. If they are unmuted, the bot mutes them. If they are already muted, it unmutes them.
moderation.muteStep2: Enable the Voice Mute option in the moderation section.
moderation.muteStep3: Save and test with !mute @user. You need the Mute Members permission and the target must be in a voice channel.

roles.title: Role Assignment
roles.heading: Assigning & Removing Roles
roles.intro: The Assign/Remove Role option toggles a role on the user who triggers the command. If they don't have the role, it's added. If they already have it, it's removed. This works with message commands, interaction buttons, and event-based commands like Member Join.
roles.toggleDesc: A command that lets users give themselves (or remove) a role.
roles.toggleStep2: Enable Assign/Remove Role and enter the role name or ID in the Role field (e.g. Red). This is case-sensitive.
roles.toggleStep3: Add a channel message to confirm the action.
roles.toggleStep4: Save and test. The bot needs the Manage Roles permission and its role must be higher than the role it's assigning.
roles.joinDesc: You can also assign a role automatically when someone joins your server. Create a Member Join command with Assign/Remove Role enabled. Since a joining member never has the role yet, it will always be added. See the Welcome Messages tutorial for the full setup.
roles.buttonDesc: For a more modern approach, use an interaction command with buttons. Each button can assign a different role when clicked. Enable Assign/Remove Role on each button's action and set the corresponding role name. See the Interactive Buttons tutorial for how to set up buttons.

reactions.title: Reactions
reactions.heading: Adding & Responding to Reactions
reactions.intro: There are two ways to use reactions in Bot Commander: making your bot add a reaction to a message as part of a command response, and creating commands that trigger when a user reacts to a message.
reactions.addDesc: Any command can make the bot react to the triggering message. This is useful for acknowledgment or fun responses.
reactions.addStep2: Enable the React option and enter the emoji name. For default emojis, use the emoji directly. For custom server emojis, use the emoji name without colons.
reactions.addStep3: Save and test. The bot will add the reaction to the message that triggered the command.
reactions.roleDesc: A popular feature that lets users react to a specific message to gain or remove a role. This uses the Reaction Add command type.
reactions.roleStep1: Create a command and set the type to Reaction Add.
reactions.roleStep2: Set the command name to the emoji that will trigger the role. For default emojis use the emoji itself (e.g. ⭐). For custom emojis, use the name without colons.
reactions.roleStep3: Enable Require Specific Message and paste the message ID of the message users will react to. To get a message ID, enable Developer Mode in Discord settings, then right-click the message and select Copy Message ID.
reactions.roleStep4: Enable Assign/Remove Role and enter the role name (e.g. Announcements).
reactions.roleStep5: Save and host. React to the message with the emoji to gain the role, react again to remove it.
reactions.roleStep6: Repeat for each role you want to assign. Create one command per emoji/role combination.

javascript.title: Using JavaScript
javascript.heading: JavaScript Eval Blocks
javascript.intro: For advanced logic beyond what keywords and conditionals offer, you can run JavaScript code inside your responses using $eval...$halt blocks. The code runs like an async function in a sandboxed environment with access to Math, Date, and the persistent botState object. The return value of the block replaces it in the message.
javascript.howDesc: The code between $eval and $halt is wrapped in a function and executed. Any BCFD keywords inside the block (like $name or $messageAfterCommand) are resolved before the JavaScript runs, so you can use them as values in your code. Use return to output a result — the returned value replaces the entire $eval...$halt block in the message.
javascript.howNote: Important: Variables declared with var or let inside the block are local to that function and cannot be accessed with $get() outside it. To share data between eval blocks or with the rest of the message, use botState (which persists across commands and restarts) or $set()/$get() which store values on the VM context directly.
javascript.basicDesc: A simple greeting command that uses JavaScript to build a response.
javascript.step2: Enter the template below. The return value replaces the eval block.
javascript.mixDesc: The eval block is just one part of your message. You can put regular text and keywords before and after it. The return value gets inserted where the block was.
javascript.advancedDesc: The botState object is available inside eval blocks and persists across commands and bot restarts. Use it to track per-user data, counters, or any state your bot needs to remember.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.metaDesc: How to use the webhooks feature in Bot Commander for Discord

using.title: Using Webhooks
using.whatText: These allow you to send messages to a certain channel using the built-in webhooks integration on Discord. You can send either a regular message or an embed.
using.howHeading: How do I create a webhook for my server?
using.howText: Go to your desired server and click on "Server Settings", from there you click on "Integrations" and then "Create Webhook". This will create the webhook for you and all you need to do is copy the webhook URL to the Bot Commander app.
